wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Han Neng portfolio website project overview. Explain the project's purpose as a modern, responsive portfolio and studio showcase website built with Next.js 16. Document its key features including interactive project displays, development notes timeline, roadmap visualization, and theme switching capabilities. Describe the target audience of indie software developers and the technology stack including TypeScript, React 19, Tailwind CSS v4, and Cloudflare Pages deployment. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners understanding what this portfolio site offers and technical details for experienced developers evaluating the architecture. Use terminology consistent with the codebase such as 'static site export', 'component composition', and 'content-driven architecture'. Provide practical examples demonstrating the site's main sections and user journey.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the core layout and infrastructure components that form the foundation of the Han Neng portfolio website. Document ThemeProvider component with its React Context implementation, theme state management, localStorage persistence, and the useTheme custom hook. Explain Navigation component's responsive design, mobile hamburger menu functionality, and integration with theme context. Detail Footer component structure, branding elements, and social media links. Include prop interfaces, TypeScript definitions, and usage examples from the actual codebase. Document how these components wrap and provide global functionality to all other components in the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the Han Neng portfolio website. Include detailed prerequisites covering Node.js installation, package manager setup (npm/yarn/pnpm/bun), and basic knowledge requirements for React and TypeScript. Provide step-by-step installation instructions including cloning the repository, installing dependencies with 'npm install', and starting the development server with 'npm run dev'. Document the local development workflow, file structure navigation, and how to make your first edit using the hot reload feature. Include troubleshooting tips for common setup issues and browser compatibility requirements. Make the content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced developers to quickly get up and running.

wiki_catalogs.name: Section Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the main page section components that compose the portfolio website layout. Document Hero component with animated text effects and call-to-action buttons. Detail Projects component with project filtering (Live/Building), grid layout, and data integration from content/projects.ts. Explain Notes component with chronological timeline organization and monthly grouping. Document Roadmap component with Now/Next/Future column visualization and status indicators. Detail About component with personal biography, skills overview, and social media integration. Document Stats component with animated counters using Intersection Observer API. Include prop interfaces, data structures, animation implementations, and responsive design patterns for each section component.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Han Neng portfolio website. Describe the high-level design using Next.js App Router with static site export configuration. Document the component-based architecture pattern with clear separation between presentation components (Hero, Projects, Notes) and utility components (ThemeProvider, ProjectCard). Explain the data flow from static content files through TypeScript interfaces to React components. Detail the context-based state management for theme switching without external dependencies. Include system boundaries showing how the frontend communicates with external services like GitHub and social media platforms. Address cross-cutting concerns like responsive design patterns, accessibility considerations, and performance optimization strategies. Document the technology stack decisions and their trade-offs for a static portfolio site.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for reusable utility components used throughout the portfolio website. Document ProjectCard component with its props interface including project metadata display, status badges, tag rendering, and external link handling. Detail BrandIcons component with custom SVG icon implementations and consistent styling patterns. Include TypeScript interfaces, prop validation, and usage examples showing how these components are composed within larger section components. Document styling approaches using Tailwind CSS classes and accessibility considerations for interactive elements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Components Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive component reference documentation for all UI components in the Han Neng portfolio website. Document each component's props, events, and customization options with concrete code examples from the actual implementation. Explain the component hierarchy starting from ThemeProvider wrapping all components, through layout components like Navigation and Footer, to section components like Hero, Projects, Notes, Roadmap, About, and Stats. Detail utility components like ProjectCard and BrandIcons with their reusable patterns. Include prop interfaces, default values, and type definitions. Document component composition patterns and how components work together to create the complete page layout. Address styling approaches using Tailwind CSS classes and responsive design implementations. Provide guidelines for extending or modifying existing components while maintaining consistency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content management documentation for the Han Neng portfolio website. Explain the content-driven architecture where all dynamic content is separated from presentation logic in dedicated TypeScript files. Document the data models and interfaces used for projects, development notes, and roadmap items. Provide step-by-step guides for adding new projects with required fields like title, description, status, tags, and links. Explain how to update development notes with chronological organization and monthly grouping. Detail the roadmap structure with Now/Next/Future categories and status indicators. Include examples of proper TypeScript interface usage and data validation. Document best practices for maintaining content consistency and organizing large amounts of information. Address how content changes are reflected in the static site build process.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling and Theming
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed styling and theming documentation for the Han Neng portfolio website. Explain the Tailwind CSS v4 implementation with utility-first approach and custom theme configuration. Document the dark/light theme system implemented through React Context API and localStorage persistence. Detail the global CSS variables, color schemes, and typography settings. Explain responsive design patterns using mobile-first approach with Tailwind breakpoints. Document animation implementations including animated statistics counters and smooth transitions. Include guidelines for adding custom styles while maintaining consistency with the existing design system. Address cross-browser compatibility considerations and performance optimization techniques for CSS delivery. Provide examples of creating new theme variants and customizing existing components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment and Production
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and production documentation for the Han Neng portfolio website. Document the static site export configuration using Next.js static generation for optimal performance and CDN distribution. Explain the Cloudflare Pages deployment setup including environment configuration and build process optimization. Detail SEO considerations with robots.txt and sitemap.xml configuration for search engine visibility. Document performance optimization strategies including image optimization, font loading, and bundle size analysis. Include monitoring and analytics integration points for tracking visitor engagement. Address security considerations for static sites and best practices for production deployments. Provide troubleshooting guides for common deployment issues and debugging techniques for production environments.

<blog>

# Han Neng - Indie Software Studio Website

## Project Introduction

Han Neng is a modern, static website built with Next.js 16 that serves as the digital portfolio and studio showcase for an indie software developer. The website demonstrates practical applications of AI, cybersecurity, cloud technologies, and data analytics through featured projects and development notes.

### Purpose Statement
The primary purpose is to showcase the developer's work, document their journey in building useful software, and provide insights into their current projects and future roadmap.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- Present professional portfolio and project showcase
- Document development progress and learning journey
- Demonstrate technical expertise in modern web technologies
- Provide clear navigation between different sections of content
- Maintain responsive design across all devices

### Target Audience
- Potential clients and collaborators
- Technology enthusiasts and fellow developers
- Industry professionals interested in AI and cybersecurity solutions
- Recruiters and hiring managers

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modern React-based architecture using Next.js 16 with TypeScript, implementing a component-driven approach with clean separation of concerns.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Next.js App Router] --> B[Root Layout]
    B --> C[ThemeProvider]
    C --> D[Home Page]
    D --> E[Navigation Component]
    D --> F[Hero Section]
    D --> G[Projects Section]
    D --> H[Notes Section]
    D --> I[Roadmap Section]
    D --> J[About Section]
    D --> K[Footer Section]
    
    L[Content Layer] --> M[projects.ts]
    L --> N[notes.ts]
    L --> O[roadmap.ts]
    
    G --> P[ProjectCard Component]
    P --> M
    
    style A fill:#e1f5fe
    style B fill:#f3e5f5
    style C fill:#e8f5e8
    style L fill:#fff3e0
```

### Component Breakdown

The application uses a hierarchical component structure where the root layout manages global state and theme, while individual sections are implemented as reusable components:

- **Layout Management**: Root layout handles metadata, fonts, and theme provider
- **State Management**: Theme context provides dark/light mode functionality
- **Data Layer**: Static content files separate business logic from presentation
- **UI Components**: Modular components for consistent design system

### Design Patterns

The project implements several key design patterns:

- **Component Composition**: Reusable UI components with props interfaces
- **Context API**: Global theme management without external dependencies
- **Static Data Pattern**: Content stored in TypeScript files for type safety
- **Responsive Design**: Mobile-first approach with Tailwind CSS utilities

### System Relationships

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A[User Interface] --> B[React Components]
    B --> C[Theme Context]
    B --> D[Static Content]
    D --> E[TypeScript Interfaces]
    F[Tailwind CSS] --> B
    G[Lucide Icons] --> B
    H[Next.js Build] --> I[Static Export]
    
    style A fill:#e3f2fd
    style B fill:#f1f8e9
    style C fill:#fff8e1
    style D fill:#fce4ec
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Application Root**: `app/layout.tsx`
- Configures global metadata, fonts (Geist), and theme provider
- Implements SEO optimization with OpenGraph and Twitter cards
- Sets up JSON-LD structured data for better search engine understanding

**Home Page**: `app/page.tsx`
- Composes main page sections in logical order
- Manages component hierarchy and layout structure

### Core Modules

**Theme Management**: `components/ThemeProvider.tsx`
- Implements dark/light theme switching with localStorage persistence
- Provides React Context for theme state management
- Handles hydration warnings and SSR compatibility

**Navigation System**: `components/Navigation.tsx`
- Responsive navigation with mobile menu support
- Integrates theme toggle functionality
- Smooth scrolling to page sections

**Project Showcase**: `components/Projects.tsx` and `components/ProjectCard.tsx`
- Dynamic project rendering based on status categories
- Interactive cards with hover effects and external links
- Type-safe project data structures

### Configuration Approach

**Build Configuration**: `next.config.ts`
- Static site export configuration for CDN deployment
- Optimized for performance and scalability

**Package Dependencies**: `package.json`
- Modern React 19 with Next.js 16
- Tailwind CSS v4 for utility-first styling
- Lucide React for consistent iconography
- TypeScript for type safety throughout the codebase

### External Dependencies

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Core Framework] --> B[Next.js 16]
    A --> C[React 19]
    A --> D[TypeScript 5]
    
    E[Styling] --> F[Tailwind CSS v4]
    E --> G[PostCSS]
    
    H[UI Elements] --> I[Lucide React Icons]
    
    J[Development] --> K[ESLint]
    J --> L[Worngler]
    
    style A fill:#e3f2fd
    style E fill:#f3e5f5
    style H fill:#e8f5e8
    style J fill:#fff3e0
```

### Integration Points

**Content Management**: `content/` directory
- Centralized data management for projects, notes, and roadmap
- Type-safe interfaces ensure data consistency
- Easy content updates without code changes

**SEO Integration**: 
- Automatic sitemap generation
- Robots.txt configuration
- Structured data markup for rich search results

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

The website provides a comprehensive showcase platform with multiple interactive features designed to engage visitors and effectively communicate the developer's expertise and ongoing work.

### Implementation Highlights

**Dynamic Theme System**: `components/ThemeProvider.tsx`
- Seamless dark/light mode switching with persistent user preferences
- CSS custom properties for consistent theming across components
- Client-side state management with proper SSR handling

**Responsive Navigation**: `components/Navigation.tsx`
- Mobile-first design with hamburger menu for smaller screens
- Smooth scroll navigation to page sections
- Integrated theme toggle accessible from any screen size

**Interactive Project Cards**: `components/ProjectCard.tsx`
- Hover animations and visual feedback
- Status indicators with color-coded badges
- External link integration for live projects and GitHub repositories

**Content-Driven Architecture**: `content/projects.ts`, `content/notes.ts`, `content/roadmap.ts`
- Separation of content from presentation logic
- Type-safe data structures prevent runtime errors
- Easy maintenance and content updates

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Loading
    Loading --> ThemeInitialized: Load saved theme
    ThemeInitialized --> Rendered: Mount components
    Rendered --> ThemeChanged: Toggle theme
    ThemeChanged --> Rendered: Update UI
    Rendered --> Navigation: User interaction
    Navigation --> Sections: Scroll to section
    Sections --> Rendered: Display content
    
    note right of ThemeInitialized
        Persists preference in localStorage
        Applies CSS classes to document root
    end note
    
    note right of Sections
        Projects, Notes, Roadmap, About
        Each section renders from typed data
    end note
```

The architecture emphasizes maintainability, performance, and user experience through careful separation of concerns and modern React patterns.

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [app/layout.tsx](app/layout.tsx)
- [app/page.tsx](app/page.tsx)
- [components/ThemeProvider.tsx](components/ThemeProvider.tsx)
- [components/Navigation.tsx](components/Navigation.tsx)
- [components/Hero.tsx](components/Hero.tsx)
- [components/Projects.tsx](components/Projects.tsx)
- [components/ProjectCard.tsx](components/ProjectCard.tsx)
- [content/projects.ts](content/projects.ts)
- [content/notes.ts](content/notes.ts)
- [content/roadmap.ts](content/roadmap.ts)
- [components/Notes.tsx](components/Notes.tsx)
- [components/Roadmap.tsx](components/Roadmap.tsx)
- [components/About.tsx](components/About.tsx)
- [next.config.ts](next.config.ts)

</blog>
